{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DEPTH = 3","old":"DEPTH = 2","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"        self.features = nn.Sequential(*layers)
        self.pool = nn.AdaptiveAvgPool2d(1)
        self.classifier = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Flatten(),
            nn.Linear(in_ch, NUM_CLASSES),
        )","old":"        self.features = nn.Sequential(*layers)
        feat_size = IMAGE_SIZE // (2**DEPTH)
        feat_dim = in_ch * feat_size * feat_size
        self.classifier = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Flatten(),
            nn.Linear(feat_dim, FC_HIDDEN),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            nn.Dropout(DROPOUT_RATE),
            nn.Linear(FC_HIDDEN, NUM_CLASSES),
        )","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"        return self.classifier(self.pool(self.features(x)))","old":"        return self.classifier(self.features(x))","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The optimizer/schedule change already improved early training, so the next high-value move is a cleaner CNN layout. A slightly deeper and wider conv stack with global average pooling should use the 256-step budget more effectively than the current flatten-plus-MLP head, while also simplifying the classifier and improving translation robustness.","secondary_modes":["micro","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DEPTH = 2","old":"DEPTH = 3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"        self.features = nn.Sequential(*layers)
        feat_size = IMAGE_SIZE // (2**DEPTH)
        feat_dim = in_ch * feat_size * feat_size
        self.classifier = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Flatten(),
            nn.Linear(feat_dim, FC_HIDDEN),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            nn.Dropout(DROPOUT_RATE),
            nn.Linear(FC_HIDDEN, NUM_CLASSES),
        )","old":"        self.features = nn.Sequential(*layers)
        self.pool = nn.AdaptiveAvgPool2d(1)
        self.classifier = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Flatten(),
            nn.Linear(in_ch, NUM_CLASSES),
        )","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"        return self.classifier(self.features(x))","old":"        return self.classifier(self.pool(self.features(x)))","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.1)","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The deeper GAP-based layout regressed sharply, so the safest next move is to return to the stronger compact architecture and test a small generalization improvement instead of another large structural change. Label smoothing is cheap, stable, and often improves CIFAR-10 validation loss under short fixed-step budgets without complicating the training script.","secondary_modes":["layout","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.1)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The best result so far came from the AdamW plus OneCycle setup, and the label-smoothing variant regressed. With a fixed 256-step budget, increasing batch size to cover more unique examples and nudging the peak learning rate upward is a targeted way to improve sample efficiency while keeping the strong optimizer and schedule intact.","secondary_modes":["topk","caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 48","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The current optimizer, schedule, and batch setup are working well, so the next likely bottleneck is model capacity rather than training dynamics. A modest width increase in both the convolutional trunk and MLP head keeps the architecture simple while giving the now-stronger optimization setup more representational room within the same 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["micro","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 4e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The current configuration looks sample-efficient and capacity-limited issues have already been improved, so the next low-complexity lever is peak learning-rate scale. With AdamW, OneCycle, batch size 256, and the wider model, a moderate increase in max learning rate is a plausible way to extract more progress within the same 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["summaries","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 2e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The current configuration is already strong, and the last learning-rate increase helped, so a low-complexity next step is to reduce regularization slightly. With only about one to one-and-a-bit effective epochs under the fixed-step budget, the current weight decay may be a bit strong for this wider model and larger batch, making a moderate reduction a plausible way to lower validation loss.","secondary_modes":["topk","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"        pct_start=0.08,","old":"        pct_start=0.15,","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"        div_factor=10.0,","old":"        div_factor=20.0,","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"The current gains from weight-decay tuning are nearly saturated, so the next likely bottleneck is how aggressively the 256-step OneCycle schedule uses its early budget. Shortening the warmup and starting from a less conservative initial learning rate should push more optimization work into the limited training horizon without changing the overall architecture or optimizer family.","secondary_modes":["topk","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.95)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"        pct_start=0.15,","old":"        pct_start=0.08,","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"        div_factor=20.0,","old":"        div_factor=10.0,","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"The more aggressive OneCycle warmup change regressed, so it makes sense to restore the stronger schedule and probe optimizer dynamics instead. Lowering AdamW's second beta should make moment estimates adapt faster over this very short 256-step horizon, which is a plausible way to improve early training without adding architectural complexity.","secondary_modes":["summaries","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.85, 0.95)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.95)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"The lower second beta improved results, which suggests this short 256-step regime benefits from faster optimizer adaptation. Reducing beta1 slightly is a focused follow-up that should make AdamW react more quickly to fresh gradients without disturbing the currently strong architecture, batch size, or schedule.","secondary_modes":["topk","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.05","old":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.0","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The current optimizer and learning-rate settings appear close to tuned, so a small regularization change is a reasonable next probe. A light dropout rate should be mild enough not to disrupt the short-horizon optimization while potentially improving validation loss for the wider model that now trains for more than one effective epoch.","secondary_modes":["layout","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.0","old":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.05","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The added dropout hurt badly, so the next sensible move is to return to the stronger no-dropout setup and test a cleaner capacity increase instead. Expanding only the MLP head is a narrower architectural change than widening the whole conv trunk again, and it may improve fit within the current well-tuned optimizer and schedule regime.","secondary_modes":["caching","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 4e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 320","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The larger MLP head regressed, so the safer direction is to restore the stronger head size and push more data through the fixed 256-step budget instead. A moderately larger batch, paired with a proportional peak learning-rate increase, is a plausible next step because earlier gains already showed that this workload benefits from covering more examples per optimization step.","secondary_modes":["topk","layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 6e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 384","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 320","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The last step showed that this workload still benefits from pushing more examples through the fixed 256-step budget. {"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 7e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 6e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 448","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 384","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The strongest empirical pattern remains that this fixed-step workload benefits from processing more examples per optimizer step, and the last batch-size increase still paid off. {"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 8e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 7e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 448","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The strongest and most consistent improvement trend has come from increasing effective sample coverage within the fixed 256-step budget. {"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-2","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 8e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 640","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The dominant improvement signal remains better sample coverage per fixed optimization step, and the last increase still improved validation loss materially. {"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-2","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-2","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 768","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 640","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The best-performing direction continues to be larger effective sample coverage per fixed optimizer step, and the batch-size scaling has remained productive even at the latest settings. {"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 2e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"At the current large-batch regime, the main sample-coverage gains have already been captured, while runtime has grown substantially. {"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 2e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The last change regressed validation loss, so the highest-value final move is to revert to the stronger regularization setting that produced the current best visible result.
